volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 1.5.453b - Section 1.5.519)

sections.label: Section 1.5.453b
sections.translation: They are making towards you , Pseudolus ; prepare your speech to meet the old fellow .

sections.label: Section 1.5.455
sections.original: erum saluto primum , ut aequomst ; postea ,
sections.translation: Good courage in a bad case is half the evil got over . ( Aloud , as he advances to meet them .) First , I salute my master , as is proper ; and alter that , if anything is left , that I bestow upon his neighbour .

sections.label: Section 1.5.457
sections.translation: Good day to you . What are you about ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.457b
sections.translation: About standing here in this fashion ( assuming an attitude ) .

sections.label: Section 1.5.458
sections.original: Statum vide hominis , Callipho , quam basilicum .
sections.translation: See the attitude of the fellow , Callipho ; how like that of a man of rank .

sections.label: Section 1.5.459
sections.translation: I consider that he is standing properly and with boldness .

sections.label: Section 1.5.460
sections.original: Decet innocentem qui sit atque innoxium
sections.translation: It befits a servant innocent and guileless , as he is , to be bold , most especially before his master .

sections.label: Section 1.5.462
sections.original: Sunt quae te volumus percontari , quae quasi
sections.translation: There are some things about which we wish to inquire of you , which we ourselves know and have heard of as though through a cloud of mist .

sections.label: Section 1.5.464
sections.original: Conficiet iam te hic verbis , ut tu censeas
sections.translation: He ’ ll manage you now with his speeches , so that you shall think

sections.label: Section 1.5.465
sections.original: non Pseudolum , sed Socratem tecum loqui .
sections.translation: it isn ’ t Pseudolus but Socrates [*] that ’ s talking to you . What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.466
sections.original: Itast , iam pridem tu me spernis , sentio .
sections.translation: For a long time you have held me in contempt , I know . I see that you have but little confidence in me . You wish me to be a villain ; still , I will be of strict honesty .

sections.label: Section 1.5.469
sections.original: Fac sis vocivas , Pseudole , aedis aurium ,
sections.translation: Take care , please , and make the recesses of your ears free , Pseudolus ,

sections.label: Section 1.5.470
sections.original: mea ut migrare dicta possint quo volo .
sections.translation: that my words may be enabled to enter where I desire .

sections.label: Section 1.5.471
sections.original: Age loquere quidvis , tametsi tibi suscenseo .
sections.translation: Come , say anything you please , although I am angry at you .

sections.label: Section 1.5.472
sections.translation: What , you , a slave , angry at me your master ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.472b
sections.translation: And does that seem wonderful to you ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.473b
sections.translation: Why , by my troth , according to what you say , I must be on my guard against you in your anger , and you are thinking of beating me in no other way than I am wont to beat yourself .

sections.label: Section 1.5.476
sections.translation: What do you think ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.476b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I think that he ’ s angry with good reason , since you have so little confidence in him .

sections.label: Section 1.5.477b
sections.translation: I ’ ll leave him alone then . Let him be angry : I ’ ll take care that he shall do me no harm . But what do you say ? What as to that which I was asking you ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.479b
sections.translation: If you want anything , ask me .

sections.label: Section 1.5.480
sections.original: quod scibo , Delphis tibi responsum dicito .
sections.translation: What I know , do you consider given you as a response at Delphi .

sections.label: Section 1.5.481
sections.original: Advorte ergo animum et fac sis promissi memor .
sections.translation: Give your attention then , and take care and please mind your promise . What do you say ? Do you know that my son is in love with a certain music-girl ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.483b
sections.translation: Yea , verily [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.5.483c
sections.translation: Whom he is trying to make free ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.484
sections.translation: Yea , verily and indeed .

sections.label: Section 1.5.484b
sections.translation: And you are scheming by cajolery and by cunning tricks to get twenty minae in ready money out of me ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.486b
sections.translation: I , get them out of you ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.487
sections.original: Ita , quas meo gnato des , qui amicam liberet ?
sections.translation: Just so ; to give them to my son , with which to liberate his mistress . Do you confess it ? Speak out .

sections.label: Section 1.5.488b
sections.original: καὶ τοῦτο ναί , καὶ τοῦτο ναί .
sections.translation: Yea , verily ; yea , verily .

sections.label: Section 1.5.489
sections.translation: He confesses it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.489b
sections.translation: Didn ’ t I tell you so just now , Callipho ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.490
sections.translation: So I remember .

sections.label: Section 1.5.490b
sections.original: Quor haec , tu ubi rescivisti ilico ,
sections.translation: Why , directly you knew of these things , were they kept concealed from me ? Why wasn ’ t I made acquainted with them ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.491b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you : because I was unwilling that a bad custom should originate in me , for a servant to accuse his master before his master .

sections.label: Section 1.5.494
sections.original: Iuberes hunc praecipitem in pistrinum trahi .
sections.translation: Wouldn ’ t you order this fellow to be dragged head first to the treadmills [*] ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.495
sections.translation: Has he done anything amiss , Simo ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.495b
sections.translation: Yes , very much so .

sections.label: Section 1.5.496
sections.original: Desiste , recte ego meam rem sapio , Callipho ;
sections.translation: Be quiet , I quite well understand my own affairs , Callipho . Is this a fault ? Now then , give your attention to the reason why I you kept ignorant of this amour . I knew that the treadmill was close at hand , if I told you .

sections.label: Section 1.5.500
sections.original: Non a me scibas pistrinum in mundo tibi ,
sections.translation: And didn ’ t you know , as well , that the treadmill would be close at hand when you kept silent on it ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.501b
sections.translation: I did know it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.501c
sections.translation: Why wasn ’ t it told me ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.502
sections.original: Quia illúd malum aderat , istuc aberat longius ;
sections.translation: The one evil was close at hand , the other at a greater distance ; the one was at the moment , the other was a few days off .

sections.label: Section 1.5.504
sections.original: Quid nunc agetis ? nam hinc quidem a me non potest
sections.translation: What will you be doing now ? For assuredly the money cannot be got in this quarter out of me , who have especially detected it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.506
sections.original: ne quisquam credat nummum , iam edicam omnibus .
sections.translation: I shall forthwith give notice to all that no one is to trust him the money .

sections.label: Section 1.5.507
sections.original: Numquam edepol quoiquam supplicabo , dum quidem
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ ll never go begging to any person , so long , at all events , as you shall be alive ; troth , you shall find me the money ; and as for me , I shall take it from you .

sections.label: Section 1.5.509b
sections.translation: You , take it from me ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.509c
sections.translation: Undoubtedly .

sections.label: Section 1.5.510
sections.original: Excludito mi hercle oculum , si dedero .
sections.translation: Troth , now , knock out my eye , if I do find it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.510b
sections.translation: You shall provide it . I warn you then to be on your guard against me .

sections.label: Section 1.5.511b
sections.translation: By my troth , I know this for sure ; if you do take it away , you will have done a wonderful and a great exploit .

sections.label: Section 1.5.513
sections.translation: I will do it , however .

sections.label: Section 1.5.513b
sections.translation: But if you don ’ t carry it off ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.513c
sections.translation: Then flog me with rods . But what if I do carry it off ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.514b
sections.translation: I give you Jupiter as your witness ,

sections.label: Section 1.5.515
sections.translation: that you shall pass your life free from punishment .

sections.label: Section 1.5.515b
sections.translation: Take care and remember that .

sections.label: Section 1.5.516
sections.original: Egon ut cavere nequeam , cui praedicitur ?
sections.translation: Could I possibly be unable to be on my guard , who am forewarned ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.517
sections.original: Praedico , ut caveas . dico , inquam , ut caveas . cave .
sections.translation: I forewarn you to be on your guard . I say you must be on your guard , I tell you . Keep watch . Look , now , with those same hands will you this day give me the money

sections.label: Section 1.5.519
sections.original: Edepol mortalem graphicum , si servat fidem .
sections.translation: By my troth , ’ tis a clever mortal if he keeps his word .
